clinical_history: 90 year old female admitted with increased shortness of breath who ruled out for acute myocardial infarction.  The ECG shows which ONE of the following?
diagnosis: This ECG shows underlying sinus mechanism with an atrial rate of about 88 beats per minute and a narrow complex junctional escape rate of less than 55/min with variable PR intervals due to probable complete AV heart block. In addition, there are multiform premature ventricular complexes. The supraventricular beats show a narrow QRS complex and a long QT with nonspecific ST-T changes. The patient underwent successful implantation of a dual-chamber pacemaker for presumed intrinsic, degenerative conduction disease, with high grade AV block. See Case 243 for a comparison of an ECG with isorhythmic AV dissociation.
